blocks.value: Futures Copy Trading lets you automatically mirror the leveraged trades of professional futures traders on 1Max. By copying a trader, their positions including any leverage used are reflected in your account, allowing you to participate in the futures market even without advanced trading knowledge.

faqs.question: 2. What is the minimum deposit to start a Future Copy Trade?

blocks.value: You need to deposit at least 10 USDT into your future copy trading account before you start copying.

faqs.question: 3. Can I manage my funds while copy trading?

blocks.value: Yes, you can add or withdraw funds at any time. Your total balance must stay above your initial investment to continue copying trades.

faqs.question: 4. What can cause a copy trade to fail?

blocks.value: In certain situations, copy trades might not go through because of platform safeguards or trading restrictions.
Common reasons include:

value: Your Balance Is Not Enough – You don’t have enough funds in your account to execute the trade.
The Order Size Is Too Small – The amount you are trying to copy is below the platform’s minimum order size.
Price Spread Difference Is Too Large – The difference between the trader’s price and the market price exceeds the allowed slippage.
Token Copy Limit Reached – The maximum amount of a token that can be copied on the platform has already been reached.
Partially Filled Orders – Only fully completed buy orders are copied; partially filled or canceled orders will not be executed in your account.

faqs.question: 5. Is my copy trading account linked to my main spot account?

blocks.value: No. Your copy trading funds are kept separate from your main spot account.

value: Fixed Amount: Each copied trade uses a fixed margin amount set by the follower.

blocks.value: images/image.png

value: Fixed Ratio: The follower’s position size is calculated proportionally based on the trader’s position size.

blocks.value: images/4deca5b5-7aed-41e2-993a-508b83e94a43.png
The actual copied size is also affected by minimum order requirements and available margin.

faqs.question: 7. Are there any fees for Copy Trading?

blocks.value: Fees are deducted automatically and there is no additional service fee. However, you need pay:

value: How many traders can I follow at the same time?You can follow up to 10 traders simultaneously. This limit ensures smooth trade execution and makes it easier to manage your portfolio.
As a contract carrier, how many users can follow me at the same time?Currently, we only allow each trader can be followed by up to 100 users simultaneously.
How do I start copy trading as a trader?

faqs.question: 1. Go Futures → Copy → Click ‘Start Taking Orders’.

blocks.value: images/image 1.png

2. After clicking ‘Start Taking Orders’, fill in the required information such as ‘Project Description’, ‘Order Amount’, and ‘Profit-Sharing Ratio’. Then, read the notes carefully and tick the User Service Agreement before submitting.
images/image 2.png

faqs.question: 3. All your trading performance & records will displayed at here, unless you delete your project

blocks.value: images/image 3.png

value: After you become a contract carrier, your futures account will enter ‘Carry Mode.’ Any trades you open will be automatically copied by your followers. If you want to trade independently, simply turn off ‘Carry Mode’ to return to normal mode.

blocks.value: images/image 4.png

value: How do I start as a contract copy user? 1. Go Futures → Copy → Select Trader & click ‘Copy’

blocks.value: images/image 5.png

faqs.question: 2.Users can choose between 2 copy modes. First is ‘Fixed Ratio’,followers copy trades by a fixed proportion of the lead trader’s position.

blocks.value: Example: If the lead trader opens 1 BTC and the follower’s ratio is 50%, the follower opens 0.5 BTC.
images/image 6.png
Second is ‘Fixed Amount’, Followers copy trades with a fixed trade size, regardless of the lead trader’s position size.
Example: The follower always opens 100 USDT per trade, even if the lead trader opens 1 BTC or 2 BTC.
images/image 7.png

4. For new users, Fixed Ratio Copy Mode is recommended because it automatically adjusts trades based on the follower’s account size, making it safer and easier to manage. Fixed Amount Copy Mode copies trades with a set size regardless of the lead trader’s position, which gives more control but can be riskier for beginners.
Future Copy Trading Disclaimer
Futures copy trading involves significant risk and does not guarantee profits. Past performance is not indicative of future results. Trades may be executed automatically without manual confirmation.

The platform does not provide investment or trading advice and makes no guarantees regarding trader performance. All trading decisions, profits, and losses arising from futures copy trading are the sole responsibility of the user. Users should assess their financial situation and risk tolerance before participating.
